title: Transnational Criminal Organization Illicit Spotter Prevention and Elimination Act
short_title: Transnational Criminal Organization Illicit Spotter Prevention and Elimination Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
plain_summary: This bill makes it a crime to help criminals at the border by telling them where police and border agents are located. It also makes it illegal to break or damage things like fences, cameras, and other equipment that help protect the border. People who do these things can go to prison for up to 10 years. If someone uses a gun while helping criminals cross the border illegally, they can get an even longer prison sentence of up to 20 years.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Transnational Criminal Organization Illicit Spotter Prevention and Elimination Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Unlawfully Hindering Immigration, Border, and Customs Controls
sections.text: This section creates new crimes and punishments. First, it makes illegal spotting a crime. Illegal spotting means telling someone where law enforcement officers are located or what they are doing, if you do this to help someone break federal laws about immigration, customs, drugs, agriculture, money, or border controls. If you do this on purpose, you can be fined and sent to prison for up to ten years. Second, it makes destroying border controls a crime. This means damaging or destroying fences, barriers, sensors, cameras, or other devices the federal government uses to control the border or entry points without permission. It also means building tunnels or structures to get around these security devices. If you do this, you can be fined and sent to prison for up to ten years. If you use or carry a gun while doing this, you can be sent to prison for up to twenty years. Third, if you try to do these crimes or plan with others to do them, you will get the same punishment as if you actually did them. This section also changes existing gun laws to include alien smuggling crimes. Alien smuggling means helping people enter the country illegally. Now, if someone uses a gun while committing alien smuggling crimes, they will face the same extra penalties that already exist for using guns during violent crimes or drug crimes. The law defines several important terms. An alien smuggling crime is any serious crime under specific immigration laws. Brandishing a gun means showing it to scare someone, even if they cannot see it clearly. A crime of violence is a serious crime that involves using force against people or property or has a high risk of violence. A drug trafficking crime is any serious crime under drug laws. Finally, this section makes technical changes to other parts of the law to match these new rules. It also extends the time limit for prosecuting these border control crimes to ten years instead of the usual five years.
